text.title: Visibility of System Status Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every visibility-of-system-status application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Provide visible feedback within 100ms of any user action. 1-3s → spinner; 3-10s → indeterminate progress indicator; over 10s → determinate progress bar with percentage AND cancel option.
rule.rationale: Below 100ms feels instant; up to 1s preserves flow; thresholds anchored in human perception research.

rule.statement: For content-heavy known-layout pages, use skeleton screens, NOT spinners. Spinners on list pages increase perceived wait time.
rule.rationale: Skeleton screens set accurate spatial expectations; spinners with unknown duration increase anxiety.

rule.statement: Every async-trigger element MUST implement three states: loading (disabled + indicator), success (confirmation), error (message with recovery action).
rule.rationale: Missing any state produces dead-click or silent-failure patterns.

rule.statement: Disable the trigger element immediately on click during async; re-enable on success or error. Never leave it active during processing.
rule.rationale: Active triggers during async cause duplicate API calls and duplicate payments.

rule.statement: aria-live=polite for non-critical status (success toast). aria-live=assertive ONLY for critical errors requiring immediate attention. role=status for persistent status regions. aria-busy=true on loading elements.
rule.rationale: Misused assertive regions interrupt users disruptively; the rubric prevents that.

rule.statement: Success toasts auto-dismiss after 3-5s. Error toasts require manual dismissal — auto-dismiss on error is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Errors users must read + act on cannot disappear before they finish reading.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
